profile.name: Michael
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; divorced single parent managing household finances for three dependents while working full-time in sales.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable discussing investments and account types, but tends to focus on outcomes (cash flow, safety, taxes) more than the mechanics; may not recall every detail without statements.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Behaviorally aggressive in markets (willing to tolerate volatility and try higher-growth ideas), but emotionally wants 'not losing what I’ve built'—so he can swing between pushing for upside and asking for capital-preservation guardrails, especially when thinking about the kids and health.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and fast-paced; uses filler like 'so' and 'kind of'; will say 'I think' or 'roughly' when unsure; prefers a clear bottom-line summary and a short list of next steps; may jump ahead to conclusions and then ask for confirmation.
emotional_tendencies: defensive if he feels judged about past decisions
relieved when there’s a clear plan
profile.decision_making_style: Decides quickly once options are framed with tradeoffs; likes 2–3 choices with a recommendation; may ask for a day to think if it affects monthly cash flow or the kids.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to share details and take guidance, but expects transparency on fees, taxes, and 'why this is better' before committing.
internal_biases: recency bias (recent market moves influence comfort level)
loss aversion around principal despite stated aggressiveness
mental accounting (separates 'cash' from 'investments' and is reluctant to touch cash)
hidden_concerns: worries about health impacting ability to work consistently
concerned about keeping the mortgage manageable while supporting three kids
fear of making a mistake that reduces financial security post-divorce
